description: Build a security ownership map from git history (people/files/edges CSV + summary.json + co-change graph; read-only).
rules: read-only — only reads `git log` from REPO; never mutates the repo
output directory is record_store; --out is pointed there
co-change graph on by default; ignores supernode commits (>50 files) and glue files (lockfiles/.github/editor config)
community detection + GraphML need networkx; the porter passes --no-communities when networkx is absent so summary.json is still produced
usage: Set REPO (the git repo dir to analyze). Output: summary.json (plus people.csv/files.csv/edges.csv/cochange_edges.csv, and communities.json/cochange.graph.json when networkx is present) under record_store.
limitation: Read-only: analyzes git history only. Communities and GraphML require networkx; without it those artifacts are skipped. Large repos may need --since/--until narrowing.
